China’s smartphone sales declined -13% y/y during the 618 promotional period, mainly due to price hikes driven by higher memory prices    Huawei led the market w/ a 21% share, and it was the only major brand to achieve positive y/y growth during the 618 period    $AAPL began its promotional campaign about one month ahead of the 618 day, offering total savings of up to CNY 2,000 on the iPhone 17 Pro series thru a combo of official discounts, platform promotions & trade-in incentives. The price cuts quickly translated into stronger sales, lifting Apple to the No.2 spot in the market. Despite the sequential rebound, sales were still down -9% y/y, primarily due to the more aggressive promotional activity for the iPhone 16 series in the same period last year    CounterPoint: The 618 shopping festival, combined with the first wave of post-college-exam upgrades, drove a month-on-month recovery in June. However, the market is expected to enter a seasonal slowdown afterward. Frequent signals from smartphone vendors and supply chain partners have made it clear to consumers that price hikes are likely to persist in the second half of the year. In such a situation, users with genuine upgrade demands tend to bring forward their purchases to the 618 period. At the same time, smartphone OEMs have adopted a profit-first and conservative strategy, further scaling back shipment plans for the year. We expect the overall Chinese smartphone market to post a double-digit decline in shipments for the full year.    $FXI $QCOM

Alleged leaks around Apple’s iPhone 18 Pro parts list are sparking renewed discussion across the semiconductor supply chain.    US models reportedly continue to rely on the full Qualcomm $QCOM modem stack, while Apple’s in-house C2 solution is limited to international units.    Despite years of investment following the Intel modem acquisition, Apple still appears to face challenges with full mmWave integration at scale.    This dynamic reinforces Qualcomm’s continued positioning in high-performance connectivity, an area many previously expected to fade over time.    At the same time, it highlights how deeply intertwined the ecosystem remains with Apple $AAPL, rather than a clean break.    What was once viewed as a declining business segment is instead showing structural persistence.    And in parallel, it gives Qualcomm’s broader strategic pivot more time to develop.
